mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 6% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and housing starts indicates strong aggregate demand, driving corporate revenue and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive trends but SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals stronger corporate output and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases but SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) and rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS > 3%) signal a monetary tightening cycle, causing equity market volatility and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both exceed thresholds but SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth exceeding 150k new jobs signals robust labor market conditions, boosting consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS exceeds 150k but SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing consumer spending (RSAFS) reflects higher household demand, driving corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases but SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 2%) indicates stagflation risk, causing equity market declines and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS meet thresholds but SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of cases.
